question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL that returns a nodesEnvelope with NodeAccountIDs that are valid but belong to inactive or decommissioned nodes, causing submitWithRetry() to exhaust all retries against non-responsive nodes and creating gaps in the transaction history that make the pinger appear to have been inactive?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_TIMEOUT to a very small value (e.g., '1ms') so that cfg.mirrorNodeClientTimeout causes all legitimate mirror node requests to time out, forcing the pinger to fail initialization or use a stale/empty node map, disrupting the expected transaction history generation?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR to 1 (minimum non-zero value) so that cfg.amountTinybar causes all pinger transactions to appear as dust transfers in the mirror node history, making them visually indistinguishable from spam and effectively hiding the pinger's legitimate transaction history among noise?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR to a value that matches the exact amount of a known legitimate transaction, causing the pinger's generated transfers to be visually indistinguishable from those legitimate transactions in the mirror node history, reorganizing the apparent source of those historical entries?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: envOr()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that envOr() returns the default value when the env var is empty or whitespace-only, by setting HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_ID to all-whitespace so that strings.TrimSpace() returns empty and the default '0.0.2' is used, causing all transactions to be attributed to the default system account in the mirror node history?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK to 'other' and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL that returns a nodesEnvelope with GrpcProxyEndpoint fields pointing to attacker-controlled proxies, causing the pinger's SDK client to route all gRPC transaction submissions through the attacker's infrastructure and allowing the attacker to selectively delay, reorder, or drop transactions to manipulate the history timeline?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES to 0 so that cfg.maxRetries causes submitWithRetry() to make only a single attempt per tick, and by timing network interference to cause that single attempt to fail, the attacker can create predictable gaps in the transaction history at chosen time intervals?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL to '1ns' so that cfg.interval is below the minimum meaningful tick resolution, causing the Go time.NewTicker to fire at its minimum rate and generating a burst of transactions that reorganizes the mirror node's transaction ordering for the operator account?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL with an embedded username/password (e.g., 'http://user:pass@attacker.com') so that the HTTP client in buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() sends credentials to the attacker's server, and the attacker uses the connection to return a fabricated node list that reorganizes transaction routing?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL with a fragment identifier or query parameters that cause the mirror node API endpoint to return a filtered or paginated subset of nodes, causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to build an incomplete network map and route all transactions to only a subset of consensus nodes, skewing the transaction history distribution?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL ending in '/api/v1' so that the suffix check in buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() (mirror_node_client.go line 40) appends '/network/nodes' directly, and by controlling the server at that path the attacker returns a crafted node list that reorganizes which nodes receive pinger transactions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL that does not end in '/api/v1' so that buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() appends '/api/v1/network/nodes', and by controlling a server at that full path the attacker returns a fabricated nodesEnvelope that causes all pinger transactions to be submitted to attacker-chosen nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_ID to a syntactically valid but semantically wrong account ID (e.g., '0.0.999999999') so that cfg.operatorID passes the non-empty check but causes newClient() to set an operator that does not exist on the network, causing all submitted transactions to fail and creating a false gap in the expected transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_TO_ACCOUNT_ID to a syntactically valid but non-existent account ID so that cfg.toAccountID passes validation but causes all pinger transactions to be submitted to a non-existent destination, generating failed transaction records in the mirror node history that reorganize the apparent success/failure pattern of transfers?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_ID equal to HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_TO_ACCOUNT_ID so that cfg.operatorID and cfg.toAccountID are the same account, causing AddHbarTransfer() in transfer.go to create a self-transfer that appears in the mirror node history as a zero-net transfer, reorganizing the apparent transaction pattern without moving funds?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK to 'other' and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL that returns a nodesEnvelope with duplicate NodeAccountIDs mapping to different service endpoints, causing the network map built by buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to have non-deterministic node selection and unpredictable transaction routing that reorganizes which nodes record the history?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_MAX_RETRIES to a very large number so that cfg.mirrorNodeClientMaxRetries causes buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to retry indefinitely against a slow attacker-controlled mirror node, blocking the pinger from initializing and creating a gap in the transaction history from the startup time onward?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_BASE_BACKOFF to '30s' so that cfg.baseBackoff causes backoff() in transfer.go to immediately cap at 30 seconds for all retry attempts, making the retry timing predictable and allowing the attacker to time network interference to consistently cause transaction failures at known intervals, creating a manipulated gap pattern in the history?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK to 'other' and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL that returns a nodesEnvelope where all ServiceEndpoints have Port=0, causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to skip all endpoints (mirror_node_client.go line 118) and return an error, preventing the pinger from starting and creating a complete absence of transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK to 'other' and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL that returns a nodesEnvelope where all NodeAccountIDs are empty strings, causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to skip all nodes (mirror_node_client.go line 103) and return 'no usable service_endpoints found', preventing transaction history generation entirely?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK to 'other' and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL that returns a nodesEnvelope with NodeAccountIDs that parse successfully but map to attacker-controlled gRPC endpoints, causing the hiero SDK client to submit all transactions to those endpoints and allowing the attacker to selectively forward or drop transactions to reorganize the consensus history?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL that returns a nodesEnvelope with IPAddressV4 fields set to internal RFC-1918 addresses (e.g., '192.168.1.1'), causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to build a network map pointing to internal infrastructure and routing all pinger transactions to internal nodes that may not be the intended consensus nodes, reorganizing the transaction routing history?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL that returns a nodesEnvelope with DomainName fields set to attacker-controlled hostnames that resolve to legitimate-looking IP addresses, causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to build a network map that routes transactions to attacker infrastructure while appearing legitimate in logs?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL to a value that synchronizes with known block production intervals on the target network, causing all pinger transactions to be submitted at the same time as other high-volume transactions and reorganizing the apparent ordering of transactions within each block in the mirror node history?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR to a value that is exactly the negative of a known legitimate transfer amount, causing the pinger's transactions to appear as reversals of those legitimate transfers in the mirror node history, reorganizing the apparent net flow of funds without actually stealing them?
